llmpipe
=======

This is a self-contained folder for experiments with LLMs for semantic parsing to logic.

The recommended files for experimenting with LLM as a parser are the following (configurations at the beginning of python files):

* `nlpsimplecollect.py`: cycle through texts in a file (default `nlpsimpletext.py`) and ask LLM to parse, storing results in a file. Usable for gpt and claude.
* `nlpsimpleconv.py`: take the file generated by `nlpsimplecollect.py` (default `nlpsimpletext_gpt_llmresults.txt`) and generate a new file, parsing / fixing raw LLM output
* `nlpsimpleprompt1.txt`: used by `nlpsimplecollect.py` as a system prompt
* `nlpsimpletext.py`: example input file used by `nlpsimplecollect.py` 
* `nlpsimpletext1.py`: another example input file for `nlpsimplecollect.py` 

The other files are examples for more complex LLM parsing (again, configurations at the beginning of the python files) and are essentially updates for the folder `../gpt/` in the same `nlpsolver` repository.

* `checkprompt.py`: just check whether json examples in a prompt file look syntactically ok and give some stats
* `nlpcollectgpt.py`: read a file of [NL, ...] triples list and call LLM to parse with the prompt from a file.
* `nlpconvcollected.py`: read a file created by `nlpcollectgpt.py` and convert to a file usable by `nlptest.py`
* `nlptest.py`: read a file of [NL,expected label,logic] triples list and call nlpsolver on each. Use with the `--solveparsed` flag 

The following folders are for more complex examples and are not yet committed:

Solver folder and reasoner
--------------------------

Python components of nlpsolver are in the `solver` folder. `nlptest.py` uses these components.

The logical reasoner used by nlpsolver is in the main (current) folder, as these files:
`gk`, `gk_name_number.txt`, `gk_taxonomy_packed.txt`, `axioms_std.js`.



Results archive
---------------

In the `testresults` folder:

Hans tests by `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219` and `logifyprompt6.txt` with max_tokens=20000 (probably not important for claude):

* tests_hans_claude_prompt6_parseresults_converted.txt
* tests_hans_claude_prompt6_parseresults.txt 

Hans tests by `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219` and `logifyprompt5.txt` with max_tokens=20000 (probably not important for claude):

* tests_hans_claude_prompt5_parseresults_converted.txt
* tests_hans_claude_prompt5_parseresults.txt 

Hans tests by `o3-mini-2025-01-31` and `logifyprompt4.txt` with max_tokens=20000 (important for o3):

* tests_hans_gpt_o3_mini_parseresults_converted.txt
* tests_hans_gpt_o3_mini_parseresults.txt

Core and Hans tests by `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219` and `logifyprompt4.txt`:

* llm_core_test_claude_parseresults_converted.txt
* llm_core_test_claude_parseresults.txt
* tests_hans_claude_parseresults_converted.py
* tests_hans_claude_parseresults.txt

Hans tests by `claude-3-7-sonnet-20250219` and `logifyprompt3.txt` (really bad):

* v1_tests_hans_claude_parseresults_converted.py
* v1_tests_hans_claude_parseresults.txt

Hans tests by `gpt-4o-2024-05-13` and `logifyprompt3.txt`:

* tests_hans_gpt_parseresults_converted.py
* tests_hans_gpt_parseresults.txt
